title: Ruth Carver's Unsolicited Observation
description: Ruth Carver, standing behind the stools and initially the most vocal civilian presence in the store, pauses her conversation with her companion and says — not to anyone in particular, her voice dropping to something almost private — 'They haven't done a single thing wrong.' She does not repeat it. She does not look at the students when she says it. But Caldwell's pencil moves. This is an unsolicited, on-the-record observation from a white civilian witness that the students' conduct has been unimpeachable — the single most useful piece of testimony the protest could generate.
